codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation that follows established project patterns. The new _get_triton_timeout_seconds() helper mirrors the AST cache's _get_parsed_source_cache_limit_bytes() pattern exactly: read env var, return default if absent, parse with try/except ValueError, clamp negatives to zero, log and fall back on invalid input. Test assertions were correctly strengthened from OR (at least one timeout set) to exact value equality checks (both == 5.0), and a new env-var override test verifies both timeouts propagate. The import os was correctly hoisted from local scope in tokenize() to module level since it is now needed by the timeout helper. All gates passed: ruff, mypy, and full pytest suite (484 passed, 14 skipped).

issues.description: No test coverage for edge cases: negative values (e.g. '-1'), empty string (''), non-numeric strings ('abc'), or extreme values ('inf'). The implementation handles all of these correctly (negative clamps to 0, empty/non-numeric fall back to default, inf parses as float('inf')), but there are no explicit tests proving it. Low risk since the function is simple and mypy-typed.

float('inf') is accepted as a valid timeout value (no upper bound clamp). If a user sets TENSOR_GREP_TRITON_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=inf, the Triton client will have an infinite timeout. This is arguably intentional (user explicitly chose it) but differs from the AST cache pattern which uses int() and would reject 'inf'. Very low practical risk.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/triton-client-api.md references the old module-level constants TRITON_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and TRITON_NETWORK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS that were replaced by _DEFAULT_TRITON_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and the _get_triton_timeout_seconds() helper. The library doc also does not mention the new TENSOR_GREP_TRITON_TIMEOUT_SECONDS env var.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/triton-client-api.md last paragraph says 'module-level constants TRITON_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and TRITON_NETWORK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS' — these no longer exist after commit fa41844.

summary: Pass. The implementation correctly makes Triton timeout configurable via TENSOR_GREP_TRITON_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 5.0s) following the same env-var pattern as the AST cache. Test assertions were strengthened from OR to exact equality on both timeout kwargs. Two non-blocking observations: (1) edge-case tests for invalid/extreme env var values are absent but the function logic is trivially correct, and (2) the triton-client-api library knowledge file is now stale.
